📍 Map Legend

Shape = Time of Day

Triangle ▲ = Nighttime (8pm–6am)
Circle ● = Daytime (6am–8pm)

Border Color = Weather Condition

Unknown weather crashes are hidden from the map.
Sky blue = Clear
Steel blue = Partly Cloudy
Medium blue = Drizzle
Dark blue = Rain
Dodger blue = Rain Showers
Dark magenta = Thunderstorm
Slate gray = Foggy
Turquoise = Snow
Dark gray = Other

Fill Color = Crash Severity

4 — Killed (Fatal)
1 — Incapacitating Injury
2 — Non-Incapacitating
3 — Possible Injury
5 — Not Injured (PDO)
0 — Unknown Severity

Hotspot Rings — Repeated Crash Locations

Pink/magenta rings mark locations where MULTIPLE crashes happened over time. Larger ring = more crashes = bigger safety problem. This color does not appear anywhere else on the map so it is impossible to confuse with severity.
Bright magenta = 5+ crashes
Major problem area — largest ring
Deep pink = 3–4 crashes
High frequency location
Hot pink = 2 crashes
Repeated location
🌐 Use layer control (bottom right) to toggle individual severity levels on/off
